label: Evidence Decay
description: Operational evidence half-life model used to flag reappraisal urgency as evidence ages.
usageNotes: This model governs reappraisal cadence, not automatic deactivation by itself.
Half-life values are defaults and may be overridden by governance for specific domains.

entries.label: Rapid Decay
entries.description: Evidence likely to become outdated quickly due to evolving technology or practice.
usageNotes: Typical for devices, endovascular techniques, biomarker panels and omics studies.

entries.label: Moderate Decay
entries.description: Evidence ages at a moderate pace and should be reviewed on a planned cadence.
usageNotes: Typical for perioperative bundles, protocols and many comparative studies.

entries.label: Slow Decay
entries.description: Evidence remains relatively stable for longer periods.
usageNotes: Typical for mature surgical risk findings and long-standing standards of care.

entries.label: Governed Decay
entries.description: Evidence lifecycle is governed primarily by explicit issuing-body updates rather than generic half-life.
usageNotes: Typical for authoritative guidelines and local protocols.
